blocks.body: An exponent tells how many times to multiply the base by itself. In b^n, b is the base and n is the exponent (or power). Example: 2^5 = 2 x 2 x 2 x 2 x 2 = 32.
Product Rule: b^m x b^n = b^(m+n). When multiplying same bases, ADD exponents. Example: x^3 x x^4 = x^7.
Quotient Rule: b^m / b^n = b^(m-n). When dividing same bases, SUBTRACT exponents. Example: x^6 / x^2 = x^4.
Power Rule: (b^m)^n = b^(m x n). When raising a power to a power, MULTIPLY exponents. Example: (x^3)^4 = x^12.
Zero Exponent: b^0 = 1 for any b not equal to 0. Example: 7^0 = 1, x^0 = 1.
Negative Exponent: b^(-n) = 1/b^n. A negative exponent means take the reciprocal. Example: 2^(-3) = 1/2^3 = 1/8.
